affordances.name: Missing evidence denominator audit
affordances.mechanism: When a coherent story is producing confidence, require the answer to audit what evidence, denominator, disconfirming case, or information source is absent before treating the picture as complete.

use_when: The available evidence forms a coherent story that feels complete.
Confidence may be tracking story quality rather than evidence completeness.
The missing denominator, disconfirming case, or absent briefing side could change the conclusion.
do_not_use_when: The answer already names the missing evidence and why its absence is random or structured.
The current decision has enough reference-class and disconfirming evidence for the commitment being made.
The packet would merely say more information may exist without naming the missing denominator.
case_evidence_needed: The coherent story or conclusion under review.
The evidence, denominator, or disconfirming case that is absent.
Whether the absence is random, incentive-shaped, or controlled by another party.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to separate story coherence from evidence completeness by naming the missing denominator, absent disconfirming case, and why the absence matters before commitment.
evidence_required: The missing denominator or absent contrary case.
The reason the absence is random, structured, or controlled by another party.
good_output_shape: Names what is missing from the picture.
Identifies whether missing evidence is structured or random.
States how the absent evidence could change the decision.

diagnostic_questions: Is this conclusion well-supported, or just well-constructed?
What evidence would need to be in the picture before confidence is warranted?
Is the missing denominator random, or is someone controlling what we see?
misuse_guards: Do not treat narrative coherence as evidence completeness.
Do not ask for generic more information; name the absent denominator or disconfirming case.
Do not let a one-sided briefing feel complete because it is internally fluent.

source_evidence.source_quote: Confidence tracks story quality, not evidence completeness.
Before we close on this, can we name the three most important things we do not know

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a missing-evidence denominator audit for coherent stories that generate confidence.
dropped_material: Did not extract separate anchoring, confirmation-bias, survivorship-bias, or base-rate cards.
Focused the record on denominator and evidence-completeness checks.
open_questions: PR46 should test whether this card improves packet evidence honesty without adding generic missing-information boilerplate.

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly says a coherent argument is not more likely to be true, so the card must not treat fluency or tidy logic as proof.

source_evidence.source_quote: A coherent argument is not more likely to be true.

absence_records.reason: The source says WYSIATI treats available evidence as complete, so the record must not promote currently visible information as the whole evidence base.

source_evidence.source_quote: it simply treats available evidence as complete
What You See Is All There Is

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 source reading found strong reviewed depth for WYSIATI as a missing-evidence denominator audit.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether the card forces specific missing-evidence checks instead of vague caution.
